title: TRIED Eval Record
description: Per-example result of one eval run on one accepted eval-set row. Eval-specific bookkeeping: run_id identifies the run, embedded EvalSpec makes the row self-contained for analysis, and each attempt's benchmark block carries the full 100-iter timing arrays needed for non-parametric paired tests (Wilcoxon signed-rank, bootstrap CIs) and IQR/quartile descriptive stats. The generator condition is identified by the parent directory `eval/results/<label>/`, not by an in-record field. The agent loop that produces these records is identical to dataset collection (same prompt, same retry budget N=5, same judge, same tolerance) — only the input set and output destination differ.

example_id.description: Matches EvalCorpusRecord.example_id. Joining key for paired McNemar / Wilcoxon tests across two label folders.

run_id.description: UUID for this whole eval run. All EvalRecords produced in one orchestrator invocation share the same run_id.

spec.description: Embedded EvalSpec. Recorded alongside the result so each row in eval/results/<model_label>/eval_rows.jsonl is self-contained for analysis (no join against eval/holdout/ required).

attempts.description: Ordered list of generator-compile-run-judge cycles. Index in the array equals attempt_n. Same shape as DatasetRow.attempts but with raw 100-iter timing arrays embedded in the benchmark block.

final_outcome.description: Terminal state for the example. Same closed vocabulary as DatasetRow.final_outcome.

final_winning_attempt_n.description: The attempt_n whose result is recorded in final_outcome. Null iff final_outcome is in {correctness_failed, all_attempts_failed}.

eval_attempt.description: One generator-compile-(run-benchmark)-judge cycle. Same shape as DatasetRow.attempts.item — intentionally, because the agent loop is identical — except the benchmark block embeds raw 100-iter timing arrays.

attempt_n.description: Zero-indexed position in the attempts array. Must equal the array index.

prior_advice_applied.description: The judge_fix_suggestion from attempt_n - 1 that this attempt acted on. Null on attempt_n == 0 or if the previous attempt yielded no suggestion.

triton_code.description: The Triton kernel source produced by the generator. Shipped to the verification server as-is.

error.description: Captured compiler error text. Null iff status == 'success'.

correctness.description: Numeric agreement between candidate Triton output and reference outputs. Null iff compile.status == 'failed'.

benchmark.description: Timing measurements, speedups, and raw 100-iter samples. Null iff correctness is null or correctness.status == 'failed'.

generator_ms.description: Wall time of the Ollama call that produced triton_code, in milliseconds.

compile_ms.description: Wall time of POST /compile on the verification server, in milliseconds. Captures Triton compile cost.

run_ms.description: Wall time of POST /run on the verification server, in milliseconds. Null if not reached (compile failed).

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this attempt started (generator call kicked off).

correctness_check.description: Candidate-vs-eager (primary) and candidate-vs-Inductor (secondary) agreement. All 10 numbers recorded per benchmarking-protocol step 5. Same shape as DatasetRow.attempts.item.correctness.

status.description: 'passed' iff vs_eager is within the tolerance_policy_used.

tolerance_policy_used.description: Key into packages/shared/src/shared/verification/tolerance.py — the source of truth for atol/rtol values and comparison semantics.

correctness_stats.description: Five-number numeric agreement summary. Same shape as DatasetRow.attempts.item.correctness.vs_eager.

eval_benchmark.description: Timing results per benchmarking-protocol.md (10 warmup + 100 timed runs, CUDA event timing, synced start/end, median reported) PLUS raw 100-iter sample arrays for downstream non-parametric stats. The median + std fields mirror DatasetRow.attempts.item.benchmark for cross-comparability with training data; the *_samples_ms arrays are the eval-only addition.

triton_ms.description: Median wall time of the candidate Triton kernel, in milliseconds.

eager_ms.description: Median wall time of PyTorch eager, in milliseconds.

inductor_ms.description: Median wall time of torch.compile + Inductor, in milliseconds.

speedup_vs_eager.description: eager_ms / triton_ms. >1.0 means Triton is faster than eager.

speedup_vs_inductor.description: inductor_ms / triton_ms. >1.0 means Triton is faster than Inductor.

triton_std_ms.description: Standard deviation of the 100 Triton timing runs, in milliseconds.

eager_std_ms.description: Standard deviation of the 100 eager timing runs, in milliseconds.

inductor_std_ms.description: Standard deviation of the 100 Inductor timing runs, in milliseconds.

triton_samples_ms.description: All 100 timed-iteration measurements for the candidate Triton kernel, in milliseconds. Required for paired non-parametric tests (Wilcoxon signed-rank), bootstrap CIs on speedup ratios, and IQR-based outlier detection from week-2 of the stats course.

eager_samples_ms.description: All 100 timed-iteration measurements for PyTorch eager, in milliseconds.

inductor_samples_ms.description: All 100 timed-iteration measurements for torch.compile + Inductor, in milliseconds.
